title: Parent phase map targets an obsolete 16-phase topology
claim: The parent specification presents a current 16-phase 000–015 execution topology that conflicts with the authoritative re-decomposed phase tree and current terminal phase sequence.
counterevidence: The 16-phase map may have been retained as historical planning context, but it is presented as the parent program's current execution map and contains no historical-only boundary.
alternativeExplanation: The parent map intentionally preserves an earlier decomposition for provenance. That remains unsafe while it is labelled as the active phase documentation map and conflicts with the authoritative manifest.
recommendation: Regenerate the parent frontmatter and phase map from manifest/phase-tree.json, or clearly archive the old map outside the active execution contract.
scopeProof: The mismatch is confined to packet documentation topology; no implementation target was modified.

focus: Correctness: phase topology authority and parent-to-child execution path
